pattern: Each column's printed width is governed by whichever entry in that column needs the most characters, so scanning column-major and tracking a running maximum length gives every column's answer in one pass.
hints: Think about each column independently — the printed width only depends on the values that live in that column.
A number's printed length is its digit count, plus one extra character whenever the number is negative.
Scan every cell once, compute each value's printed length, and keep a running maximum per column.

edge_inputs.description: mixed positive and negative values across columns
single row, extreme min and max values
negative two-digit ties a positive two-digit in the same column
all zeros still need width one, not zero
single row with several columns of differing widths

stress.description: random rectangular grid across the full value range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    rows = max(1, min(100, size // 50 + 1))
    cols = max(1, min(100, (size % 97) + 1))
    grid = [[rng.randint(-1000000000, 1000000000) for _ in range(cols)] for _ in range(rows)]
    return [grid]
